stories.title: Extreme Heat Poses Threat to Players and Fans at 2026 FIFA World Cup
stories.desc: Researchers warn that rising temperatures could threaten athletes, fans, workers and officials during the World Cup games starting in June.

stories.title: FIFA Confirms Iran Will Play 2026 World Cup in USA Despite Tensions
stories.desc: FIFA President Gianni Infantino confirms Iran will participate in the tournament and play in the United States, staying in Group G with Belgium, New Zealand and Egypt despite security concerns.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia and Senegal to Play World Cup Friendly in San Antonio
stories.desc: Toyota Field will host an international friendly between Saudi Arabia and Senegal on June 9, serving as an appetizer before the 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off on June 11.

stories.title: World Cup 2026 Drives Rising Tourism Demand Across North America
stories.desc: Hotel prices are soaring and connectivity gaps are emerging as the United States, Canada, and Mexico prepare for surging visitor demand driven by the World Cup.

stories.title: Panini Launches Biggest-Ever World Cup Sticker Album with 980 Stickers
stories.desc: The 2026 FIFA World Cup album features 980 stickers across 112 pages and launched on April 28 at Wembley Stadium with a special Coca-Cola partnership featuring 12 exclusive stickers.

stories.title: 48-Team World Cup Creates Biggest Challenge Yet for Panini Collectors
stories.desc: The decades-old football sticker craze comprises 980 unique stickers including 68 special ones in a 112-page album, with Panini hosting live swap shops across the UK in May.
